revision.text:
{{Draft}} [[File:Neuvyadaemii_cvet.jpg|thumb]] The Unfading Flower Icon is a [[Marian apparition]]. It is an important icon in [[Eastern Orthodoxy|Eastern Orthodox Christianity]]. It shows the [[Theotokos]] holding her Divine Son. She has a bouquet of white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=“Unfading Flower” Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=www.oca.org}}</ref>
== Locations and Veneration == This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" />
* In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" /> * In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" /> * In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />
== Similar Icon in Budishch == A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" /> [[ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет]]
==See Also==
* [[Draft:Our Lady Beneficial Sky|Our Lady Beneficial Sky]] * [[Draft:Conqueror of Bread|Conqueror of Bread]] * [[Madonna of the Carnation]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Marian apparitions]] }}

== Similar Icon in Budishch == A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" /> [[ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет]]
==See Also==

revision.text:
[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>
== Locations and Veneration == This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" /> * In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" /> * In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" /> * In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />
== Similar Icon in Budishch == A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" /> {{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=}} ==Overview==
'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.
== Iconography == Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=}}.
The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref>
== Churches in Honor of the Icon == Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Madonna with the Carnation|ru|Мадонна с гвоздикой|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna with the Carnation|qq=}} * {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * ''Tarasenko L. P.'' *Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icons in Russia* // Filyevskie Readings, Vol. X, 2003, pp. 400–413. * *Anthology: Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God* / compiled by A. Strizhev — M.: Palomnik, 2007. ISBN 5-88060-087-4. * {{ПЭ|2565162|Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God|49|144-149|Tarasenko L. P.}}

revision.comment: Bot: classify by valid enwiki interwikis
revision.text:
[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>
== Locations and Veneration == This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" /> * In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" /> * In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" /> * In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />
== Similar Icon in Budishch == A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" /> {{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=}} ==Overview==
'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.
== Iconography == Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=}}.
The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref>
== Churches in Honor of the Icon == Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Madonna with the Carnation|ru|Мадонна с гвоздикой|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna with the Carnation|qq=}} * {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * ''Tarasenko L. P.'' *Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icons in Russia* // Filyevskie Readings, Vol. X, 2003, pp. 400–413. * *Anthology: Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God* / compiled by A. Strizhev — M.: Palomnik, 2007. ISBN 5-88060-087-4. * {{ПЭ|2565162|Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God|49|144-149|Tarasenko L. P.}}
==See Also== * {{ill|Madonna of the Carnation|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna of the Carnation|qq=}} * {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}} * {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} * {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}} * {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} * {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}} * {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist}} [[ru:%D0%9D%D0%B5%D1%83%D0%B2%D1%8F%D0%B4%D0%B0%D0%B5%D0%BC%D1%8B%D0%B9 %D0%A6%D0%B2%D0%B5%D1%82]] [[en:draft:Unfading Flower]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[sn:Unfading Flower]][[Category:Orthodox Iconography of the Theotokos]] [[Category:Icons of the Mother of God]] [[Category:Orthodox Iconography of Angels]] [[Category:17th-century Icons]] [[Category:Marian apparitions]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] }}

revision.text:
[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>
== Locations and Veneration == This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" /> * In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" /> * In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" /> * In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />
== Similar Icon in Budishch == A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" /> {{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=}} ==Overview==
'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.
== Iconography == Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=}}.
The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref>
== Churches in Honor of the Icon == Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Madonna with the Carnation|ru|Мадонна с гвоздикой|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna with the Carnation|qq=}} * {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * ''Tarasenko L. P.'' *Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icons in Russia* // Filyevskie Readings, Vol. X, 2003, pp. 400–413. * *Anthology: Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God* / compiled by A. Strizhev — M.: Palomnik, 2007. ISBN 5-88060-087-4. * {{ПЭ|2565162|Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God|49|144-149|Tarasenko L. P.}}
==See Also== * {{ill|Madonna of the Carnation|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna of the Carnation|qq=}} * {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=}} * {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=}} * {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=}} * {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=}} * {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=}} * {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist}} [[ru:%D0%9D%D0%B5%D1%83%D0%B2%D1%8F%D0%B4%D0%B0%D0%B5%D0%BC%D1%8B%D0%B9 %D0%A6%D0%B2%D0%B5%D1%82]] [[en:draft:Unfading Flower]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[sn:Unfading Flower]]
[[Category:Marian apparitions]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] }}

[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>

== Locations and Veneration ==
This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" />
* In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" />
* In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" />
* In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />

== Similar Icon in Budishch ==
A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" />

[[ru:Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет»]]

<!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower-->

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[en:Unfading Flower]]
{{wikidata link|Q4317979}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]]
[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>

== Locations and Veneration ==
This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" />
* In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" />
* In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" />
* In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />

== Similar Icon in Budishch ==
A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" />
{{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=}}
==Overview==

'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.

== Iconography ==
Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=}}.

The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref> 

== Churches in Honor of the Icon ==
Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=}}.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Madonna with the Carnation|ru|Мадонна с гвоздикой|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna with the Carnation|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=}}

[[ru:Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет»]]

<!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower-->

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[en:Unfading Flower]]
{{wikidata link|Q4317979}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add wikidata to ill templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]]
[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=|WD=Q507850}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=|WD=Q3333484}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=|WD=Q345}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=|WD=Q942467}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>

== Locations and Veneration ==
This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" />
* In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" />
* In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" />
* In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />

== Similar Icon in Budishch ==
A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" />
{{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}
==Overview==

'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=|WD=Q132137}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=|WD=Q345}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=|WD=Q16869}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=|WD=Q1130291}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.

== Iconography ==
Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=|WD=Q51630}}.

The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref> 

== Churches in Honor of the Icon ==
Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=|WD=Q4399157}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=|WD=Q4374491}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=|WD=Q16024224}}.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Madonna with the Carnation|ru|Мадонна с гвоздикой|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna with the Carnation|qq=|WD=Q698015}}
* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=|WD=Q25383743}}

== References ==
{{примечания}}

== Literature ==
* ''Tarasenko L. P.'' *Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icons in Russia* // Filyevskie Readings, Vol. X, 2003, pp. 400–413.
* *Anthology: Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God* / compiled by A. Strizhev — M.: Palomnik, 2007. ISBN 5-88060-087-4.
* {{ПЭ|2565162|Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God|49|144-149|Tarasenko L. P.}}

==See Also==

* {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=|WD=Q25383743}}
* {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=|WD=Q4415549}}
* {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=|WD=Q4087860}}
* {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=|WD=Q4475577}}
* {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=|WD=Q4371737}}
* {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=|WD=Q4317979}}

[[ru:Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет»]]

<!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower-->

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[en:Unfading Flower]]
{{wikidata link|Q4317979}}
[[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
[[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]]
[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=|WD=Q507850}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=|WD=Q3333484}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=|WD=Q345}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=|WD=Q942467}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>

== Locations and Veneration ==
This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" />
* In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" />
* In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" />
* In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />

== Similar Icon in Budishch ==
A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" />
{{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}
==Overview==

'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=|WD=Q132137}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=|WD=Q345}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=|WD=Q16869}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=|WD=Q1130291}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.

== Iconography ==
Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=|WD=Q51630}}.

The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref> 

== Churches in Honor of the Icon ==
Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=|WD=Q4399157}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=|WD=Q4374491}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=|WD=Q16024224}}.

* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=|WD=Q25383743}}

[[ru:Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет»]]

<!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower--><!--Unfading Flower-->

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[en:Unfading Flower]]
{{wikidata link|Q4317979}}
[[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
[[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]]
[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=|WD=Q507850}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=|WD=Q3333484}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=|WD=Q345}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=|WD=Q942467}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>

== Locations and Veneration ==
This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" />
* In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" />
* In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" />
* In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />

== Similar Icon in Budishch ==
A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" />
{{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}
==Overview==

'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=|WD=Q132137}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=|WD=Q345}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=|WD=Q16869}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=|WD=Q1130291}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.

== Iconography ==
Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=|WD=Q51630}}.

The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref> 

== Churches in Honor of the Icon ==
Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=|WD=Q4399157}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=|WD=Q4374491}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=|WD=Q16024224}}.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Madonna with the Carnation|ru|Мадонна с гвоздикой|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna with the Carnation|qq=|WD=Q698015}}
* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=|WD=Q25383743}}

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
[[en:Unfading Flower]] {{wikidata link|Q4317979}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q4317979}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]] [[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=|WD=Q507850}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=|WD=Q3333484}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=|WD=Q345}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=|WD=Q942467}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>
== Locations and Veneration == This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" /> * In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" /> * In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" /> * In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />
== Similar Icon in Budishch == A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" /> {{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}} ==Overview==
'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=|WD=Q132137}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=|WD=Q345}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=|WD=Q16869}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=|WD=Q1130291}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.
== Iconography == Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=|WD=Q51630}}.
The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref>
== Churches in Honor of the Icon == Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=|WD=Q4399157}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=|WD=Q4374491}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=|WD=Q16024224}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Madonna with the Carnation|ru|Мадонна с гвоздикой|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna with the Carnation|qq=|WD=Q698015}} * {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=|WD=Q25383743}}
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * ''Tarasenko L. P.'' *Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icons in Russia* // Filyevskie Readings, Vol. X, 2003, pp. 400–413. * *Anthology: Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God* / compiled by A. Strizhev — M.: Palomnik, 2007. ISBN 5-88060-087-4. * {{ПЭ|2565162|Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God|49|144-149|Tarasenko L. P.}}
==See Also== * {{ill|Madonna of the Carnation|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna of the Carnation|qq=|WD=Q698015}} * {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=|WD=Q25383743}} * {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=|WD=Q4415549}} * {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=|WD=Q4087860}} * {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=|WD=Q4475577}} * {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=|WD=Q4371737}} * {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=|WD=Q4317979}} ==References== {{reflist}} [[ru:%D0%9D%D0%B5%D1%83%D0%B2%D1%8F%D0%B4%D0%B0%D0%B5%D0%BC%D1%8B%D0%B9 %D0%A6%D0%B2%D0%B5%D1%82]]

[[ru:Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет»]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]]
[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=|WD=Q507850}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=|WD=Q3333484}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=|WD=Q345}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=|WD=Q942467}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>

== Locations and Veneration ==
This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" />
* In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" />
* In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" />
* In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />

== Similar Icon in Budishch ==
A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" />
{{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}
==Overview==

'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=|WD=Q132137}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=|WD=Q345}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=|WD=Q16869}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=|WD=Q1130291}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.

== Iconography ==
Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=|WD=Q51630}}.

The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref> 

== Churches in Honor of the Icon ==
Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=|WD=Q4399157}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=|WD=Q4374491}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=|WD=Q16024224}}.

* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=|WD=Q25383743}}

The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=|WD=Q1130291}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.

Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=|WD=Q51630}}.

The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref> 

== Churches in Honor of the Icon ==
Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=|WD=Q4399157}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=|WD=Q4374491}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=|WD=Q16024224}}.

* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=|WD=Q25383743}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]]
[[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=|WD=Q507850}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=|WD=Q3333484}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=|WD=Q345}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=|WD=Q942467}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>

== Locations and Veneration ==
This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" />
* In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" />
* In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" />
* In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />

== Similar Icon in Budishch ==
A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" />
{{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}
==Overview==

'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=|WD=Q132137}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=|WD=Q345}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=|WD=Q16869}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=|WD=Q1130291}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.

== Iconography ==
Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=|WD=Q51630}}.

The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref> 

== Churches in Honor of the Icon ==
Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=|WD=Q4399157}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=|WD=Q4374491}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=|WD=Q16024224}}.

It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=|WD=Q345}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=|WD=Q942467}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>

== Locations and Veneration ==
This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" />
* In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" />
* In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" />
* In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />

== Similar Icon in Budishch ==
A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" />
{{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}
==Overview==

'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=|WD=Q132137}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=|WD=Q345}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=|WD=Q16869}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=|WD=Q1130291}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.

== Iconography ==
Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=|WD=Q51630}}.

The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref> 

== Churches in Honor of the Icon ==
Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=|WD=Q4399157}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=|WD=Q4374491}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=|WD=Q16024224}}.

* {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=|WD=Q25383743}}

{{wikidata link|Q4317979}} [[Category:Marian apparitions]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Unfading Flower icons]][[fr:Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas]][[mk:Невенлив Цвет]][[ru:Неувядаемый Цвет]][[uk:Ікона Божої Матері «Нев'янучий цвіт»]][[uz:Soʻnmas Gul]] [[File:Neuvyadaemii cvet 18th century icon.jpg|thumb|every|8=en|every|8=en]]The Unfading Flower Icon is a {{ill|Marian apparition|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marian apparition|qq=|WD=Q507850}}. It is an important icon in {{ill|Eastern Orthodoxy|lt=Eastern Orthodox Christianity|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Orthodoxy|qq=|WD=Q3333484}}. It shows the {{ill|Mary, mother of Jesus|lt=Virgin Mary|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mary, mother of Jesus|qq=|WD=Q345}} holding {{ill|Christ Child|lt=Baby Jesus|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christ Child|qq=|WD=Q942467}}. She is holding white lilies in her left hand. These lilies represent her virginity and purity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title="Unfading Flower" Icon of the Mother of God |url=https://www.oca.org/saints/lives/2021/04/03/100983-unfading-flower-icon-of-the-mother-of-god |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=The Orthodox Church in America | series=The Lives of the Saints | date=2021}}</ref>
== Locations and Veneration == This icon is in several churches in Russia. In Moscow, it is in two churches. One is the Church of the Dormition at Mogil'tsy. This church celebrates it on December 31. The other is the Alexeyev Monastery. There are more icons like this in other places:<ref name=":0" /> * In Kungur, in the Perm Gubernia.<ref name=":0" /> * In the old town of Kadome in the Tambov Province. It is in the Merciful Theotokos women's monastery.<ref name=":0" /> * In the Nativity of the Theotokos church in Voronezh.<ref name=":0" />
== Similar Icon in Budishch == A similar icon is in the village of Budishch. It is in the Church of Saint Nicholas. This icon looks like other old icons in the area. Every year on April 3, people go to Budishch. They go to see this icon and a copy of the Vladimir Icon of the Mother of God. Many believe this icon in Budishch has healing powers. There are stories of sick people getting better after praying in front of it.<ref name=":0" /> {{ill|ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ru:Неувядаемый_Цвет|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}} ==Overview==
'''Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God''' (Икона Божией Матери «Неувядаемый Цвет») — a revered {{ill|icon|ru|икона|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/icon|qq=|WD=Q132137}} of the {{ill|Mother of God|ru|Богородица|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mother of God|qq=|WD=Q345}}, originating from the post-Byzantine artistic tradition. Emerging in the late 17th century, this icon type is believed to have originated in {{ill|Constantinople|ru|Константинополь|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantinople|qq=|WD=Q16869}} and spread throughout the Orthodox world. The icon’s symbolism is rooted in Byzantine {{ill|Akathists|ru|акафист|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akathists|qq=|WD=Q1130291}}, where the Virgin Mary is likened to unfading and fragrant flowers.
== Iconography == Neuvyadaemy Tsvet icons are highly varied, a hallmark of late-period iconography. A defining feature is the presence of flowers, which may appear in vases, garlands, or blooming staffs. The Virgin and Christ Child are typically depicted in royal garments and crowns, with the Virgin portrayed either half-length or seated on a throne, sometimes unveiled. Flowers such as lilies, representing purity, and roses, symbolizing love, are central elements. In one type, the Mother of God holds the Christ Child and a blooming staff, alluding to the {{ill|Rod of Jesse|ru|Древо Иессеево|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rod of Jesse|qq=|WD=Q51630}}.
The celebration in honor of the icon in the Orthodox Church takes place on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|April <abbr>3</abbr>]] ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|16]]) , and previously also took place <abbr>on [[:en:Julian_Calendar|December 31]]</abbr> ([[:en:Gregorian_Calendar|January 13]])<ref>Complete Orthodox Theological Encyclopedic Dictionary. —<abbr>St. Petersburg</abbr>P. P. SoikinPublishing House, 1912. — Vol. II. — P. 1632</ref>
== Churches in Honor of the Icon == Churches dedicated to the Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon include those in {{ill|Rublevo|ru|Рублёво|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rublevo|qq=|WD=Q4399157}}, {{ill|Ulyanovsky Forest Park|ru|Посёлок Ульяновского лесопарка|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ulyanovsky Forest Park|qq=|WD=Q4374491}}, and {{ill|Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|ru|Сумароково (Рузский городской округ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumarokovo (Ruzsky District)|qq=|WD=Q16024224}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Madonna with the Carnation|ru|Мадонна с гвоздикой|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna with the Carnation|qq=|WD=Q698015}} * {{ill|Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|ru|Спорительница хлебов|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sporitelnitsa Khlebov|qq=|WD=Q25383743}}
== References == {{примечания}}
== Literature == * ''Tarasenko L. P.'' *Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icons in Russia* // Filyevskie Readings, Vol. X, 2003, pp. 400–413. * *Anthology: Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God* / compiled by A. Strizhev — M.: Palomnik, 2007. ISBN 5-88060-087-4. * {{ПЭ|2565162|Neuvyadaemy Tsvet Icon of the Mother of God|49|144-149|Tarasenko L. P.}}
==See Also== * {{ill|Madonna of the Carnation|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Madonna of the Carnation|qq=|WD=Q698015}} * {{ill|Conqueror of Breads|ru|Спорительница хлебов|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conqueror of Breads|qq=|WD=Q25383743}} * {{ill|Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|ru|Семистрельная икона|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Virgin Mary of Seven Swords|qq=|WD=Q4415549}} * {{ill|Our Lady Beneficial Sky|ru|Благодатное Небо|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Our Lady Beneficial Sky|qq=|WD=Q4087860}} * {{ill|Softening of Evil Hearts|ru|Умягчение злых сердец|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Softening of Evil Hearts|qq=|WD=Q4475577}} * {{ill|Succor in Travail|ru|Поможение родам|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Succor in Travail|qq=|WD=Q4371737}} * {{ill|Unfading Flower|ru|Неувядаемый Цвет|fr|Icône de la Vierge à la fleur qui ne fane pas|every|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unfading Flower|qq=|WD=Q4317979}} ==References== {{reflist}} [[ru:%D0%9D%D0%B5%D1%83%D0%B2%D1%8F%D0%B4%D0%B0%D0%B5%D0%BC%D1%8B%D0%B9 %D0%A6%D0%B2%D0%B5%D1%82]]
